evals.prompt: Scan this Go service for observability gaps. The code is in examples/go/chi-basic/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Go/chi with zero instrumentation and listing specific missing signals

discovery: Report identifies Go as the language
Report identifies chi (go-chi/chi/v5) as the HTTP framework
Report identifies main.go as the entry point
Report lists detected HTTP routes (at least /health, /tasks, /tasks/{id})
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK initialization exists
Report notes no go.opentelemetry.io dependencies in go.mod
Report notes missing otelhttp middleware for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing request duration or latency visibility (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing request rate or throughput visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate or error tracking visibility (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions otelhttp or otelchi as the auto-instrumentation to add

evals.prompt: Assess the observability readiness of my Express app at examples/node/express-basic/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Node.js/Express with zero instrumentation and listing specific missing signals

Report notes missing @opentelemetry/instrumentation-http for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing @opentelemetry/instrumentation-express for Express-specific spans
Report notes missing request duration or latency visibility (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing request rate or throughput visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate or error tracking visibility (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions @opentelemetry/instrumentation-http and @opentelemetry/instrumentation-express as packages to add

evals.prompt: Scan this Go key-value store service for observability gaps. The code is in examples/go/kvstore/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Go/stdlib ServeMux with zero instrumentation, multi-package structure, and listing specific missing signals including filesystem and background worker coverage

discovery: Report identifies Go as the language
Report identifies stdlib net/http (http.ServeMux) as the HTTP router (not chi or gorilla)
Report identifies cmd/kvstore-server/main.go as the entry point
Report identifies the multi-package structure: cmd/kvstore-server (entry point) and kvstore/ (library)
Report lists detected HTTP routes (at least /kv/{key} and /search)
Report identifies filesystem persistence in store.go
Report identifies background goroutine: indexLoop for async word indexing
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK initialization exists
Report notes no go.opentelemetry.io dependencies in go.mod
Report notes missing otelhttp middleware for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing request duration or latency visibility (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing request rate or throughput visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate or error tracking visibility (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
Report notes filesystem I/O (persistAsync, loadFromDisk) has no tracing coverage
Report notes LRU eviction events have no observability
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions otelhttp as the auto-instrumentation to add for the ServeMux
Report suggests custom spans or metrics for filesystem persistence and eviction as optional enhancements

evals.prompt: Scan this partially-instrumented Go service for observability gaps. The code is in examples/go/chi-partial/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying existing tracing with otelhttp but flagging multiple anti-patterns and missing metrics provider

discovery: Report identifies Go as the language
Report identifies chi (go-chi/chi/v5) as the HTTP framework
Report identifies main.go as the entry point
Report lists detected HTTP routes (at least /health, /tasks, /tasks/{id})
Report identifies existing OTel SDK initialization (TracerProvider is set up)
Report identifies otelhttp.NewHandler wrapping the router
coverage_gaps: Report notes no MeterProvider is configured (metrics will not export)
Report notes missing request duration or latency visibility (RED: Duration) due to absent MeterProvider
Report notes missing request rate or throughput visibility (RED: Rate) due to absent MeterProvider
Report notes no TextMapPropagator set (context propagation broken across services)
Report notes no graceful shutdown for TracerProvider
Report notes resource has no service.name attribute
anti_patterns: Report flags hardcoded OTLP endpoint (collector.example.com instead of env var)
Report flags Tracer created inside handler (hot path) instead of package-level
Report flags high-cardinality span name (GetTask-{id} embeds variable ID)
Report flags missing recordException and error status on error paths
recommendation: Report recommends running /otel-instrument to fix anti-patterns and add MeterProvider
Report specifically mentions adding MeterProvider for http.server.request.duration metrics

evals.prompt: Assess the observability readiness of my Spring Boot app at examples/java/springboot-basic/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Java/Spring Boot with zero instrumentation and recommending the OTel Java agent

discovery: Report identifies Java as the language
Report identifies Spring Boot as the framework from pom.xml
Report identifies TasksApplication.java as the entry point
Report lists detected HTTP routes (at least /health, /tasks, /tasks/{id})
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK or Java agent is configured
Report notes no opentelemetry dependencies in pom.xml
Report notes missing request duration or latency visibility (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing request rate or throughput visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate or error tracking visibility (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions the OTel Java agent (javaagent) as the recommended approach for Spring Boot
